blockingIssues.description: The assigned feature was to harden the representative snake_case invalid-params timeout path, but commit `ab66a27` makes no change to `tests/invalid-params.test.ts` or any app/setup code exercised by that test. The only shipped diff touches exchange-detail pair breadth, so the feature is not actually implemented in the reviewed commit.

This feature handoff points at commit `ab66a27`, whose code changes are unrelated to invalid-params timeout hardening: it swaps `/exchanges/{id}` aggregate `coins`/`pairs` to use raw ticker rows. Because the reviewed feature’s expected behavior concerns the runtime cost or timeout budget of the snake_case representative-response scan, this commit-to-feature mismatch means the reported success is unsupported by the actual shipped code.

suggestedGuidanceUpdates.suggestion: Require workers to prove the reported feature commit actually contains scoped implementation or test changes for the assigned feature before calling EndFeatureRun success, and treat commit/handoff mismatches as validation failures.
suggestedGuidanceUpdates.evidence: Review for `baseline-invalid-params-timeout-hardening` found session `46f683f5-39ae-4d91-968b-05cb66023575` reran validators and reported success on commit `ab66a27ce63c7bb9cc8fc4172472212b35f39396`, but `git show ab66a27` only changes exchange-detail breadth behavior and related exchange tests.

rejectedObservations.observation: The baseline endpoint smoke script still probes only `/ping` and does not exercise `/health`.
rejectedObservations.reason: non-blocking knowledge note for the readiness feature; current validator expectations already pass because `/health` behavior is reconciled and the smoke script remains operable against the documented externally started server flow.
